interest: Equity advocates, low-SES and Roma families, reform movements, and the digital-economy sector share an interest in ensuring that reweighting entry actually reaches disadvantaged applicants rather than merely displacing advantaged families into unregulated non-state channels.
value: Equity (fair access across all maintainers) in tension with institutional autonomy of church and foundation schools, and with the political durability of a reform that avoids middle-class mobilization.
fear: That an equity reform binding only state schools becomes a cosmetic 'reformed' label while affluent families relocate to exempt church/foundation channels, legitimising unchanged sorting and betraying groups repeatedly promised reform.
affected: Digital-economy employers relying on a stable talent pipeline
assumption: That church and foundation maintainers can be brought under uniform equity rules with enforceable sanctions within a single legislative act, without a separate and possibly unwinnable institutional/legal fight over maintainer autonomy.
empirical_uncertainty: Contested-to-unknown: cross-maintainer compliance is flagged as weak/legally-uncertain evidence (legal_and_governance maintainer-plurality finding), and whether advantaged families would in fact relocate absent binding sanctions is a plausible but unquantified behavioral prediction.
